dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the hearing of Pharaoh, saying —

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: My father made me swear, saying: Behold, I am about to die; in my tomb which I dug for myself in the land of Canaan, there you shall bury me. Now therefore, let me please go up and bury my father; then I will return.

dialogue.speaker: pharaoh-of-joseph
dialogue.englishText: Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear.

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: What if Joseph bears a grudge against us and pays us back in full for all the wrong we did to him?

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Your father gave a command before his death, saying —

dialogue.speaker: jacobs-sons
dialogue.englishText: Thus you shall say to Joseph: Please forgive the transgression of your brothers and their sin, because they did evil to you. And now, please forgive the transgression of the servants of the God of your father.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: Do not be afraid, for am I in the place of God?

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to bring it about that many people should be kept alive, as they are today.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: So do not be afraid; I will provide for you and your little ones.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: I am about to die, but God will surely visit you and bring you up out of this land to the land that he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.

dialogue.speaker: joseph
dialogue.englishText: God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.
